vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore
Vehicle context: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach
Suggested material: Al 7075 + steel + composite, process: CNC + LPBF

verify.rule: Ensure all necessary data points are included.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to harmonic drive heating.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling systems or redesign cooling fins for better heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to inadequate strain relief.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have proper strain relief geometry and follow recommended bend radii.

risk.desc: Structural integrity concerns with harmonic drive under peak torque.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough testing and simulation to validate structural integrity under maximum load conditions.

risk.desc: Complex manufacturing process may introduce variability.
risk.mit: Standardize manufacturing processes and conduct regular quality checks.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment issues with mounting flange.
risk.mit: Include alignment aids and ensure precise manufacturing tolerances.

manufacturing_strategy.process: CNC machining for aluminum parts, LPBF for complex geometries

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: CMM for dimensional checks, visual inspection for surface finish

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value
All required evidence_features must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops
Each part_tree child and geometry_op must be mutually traceable by id/name
Adjacent interfaces must have reciprocal connection_type, DOF, clearance_mm, and required_feature
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to different materials used in the joint.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as using thermal insulators between different material interfaces.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage during assembly if the slack loops are not properly managed.
risk.mit: Ensure proper training and guidelines for assembly personnel to manage cable slack effectively.

risk.desc: Complexity in manufacturing due to the combination of CNC and LPBF processes.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough process validation and simulation before full-scale production.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly if the primary datum is not accurately established.
risk.mit: Use precision tools and methods to establish the primary datum during manufacturing.

risk.desc: Potential for increased manufacturing cost due to the use of high-performance materials like Al 7075.
risk.mit: Evaluate alternative materials that provide similar performance at a lower cost.

physics_paths.description: Shoulder pitch motion path
Load transfer from payload to shoulder joint
Heat dissipation from motor to housing
Power and signal transmission from controller to motor

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate encoders and sensors within the shoulder joint for precise motion control
Optimize cable routing through the center bore to ensure minimal interference with joint movement
Enhance heat dissipation pathways from the motor to the housing to maintain optimal operating temperatures

manufacturing_strategy.process: CNC machining for aluminum components, LPBF for complex geometries
manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum on the base mounting flange
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: ±0.05mm for critical interfaces, ±0.1mm for general features
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures designed for LPBF process, orientation optimized for minimal distortion
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Coordinate measuring machine (CMM) for dimensional accuracy, visual inspection for surface finish

verification_focus.description: Verify the mass of the shoulder joint against the design specifications
Ensure all critical interfaces have appropriate clearances for smooth operation
Validate cable routing through the center bore to avoid pinch points and ensure adequate slack

generated_parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
generated_parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

requirements: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075
Transmits loads from shoulder to elbow
Cable conduit allows wiring from shoulder to elbow
Heat dissipation from link body to ambient air
Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent
Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole
Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent for inner/outer races
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range
Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links
Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels
Mass does not exceed 1.5kg
Performs FEA analysis to ensure link can handle 5kg payload
Confirm all service paths and access windows are unobstructed

generated_parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
generated_parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Driver board bay
Power/signal separation
Return path and grounding
Board mounting and clearance

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.
[power_signal_separation] good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry.
[return_path_and_grounding_intent] good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals.

verify.value: PCBs mount on standoffs with clearance
verify.rule: [board_mounting_and_clearance] good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns.
Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to insufficient heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Integrate additional heat sinks and optimize airflow paths.

risk.desc: Risk of electromagnetic interference if power and signal lines are too close.
risk.mit: Increase separation between power and signal channels.

risk.desc: Service access may be limited if the service cover is too small.
risk.mit: Expand the size of the service cover to allow better access.

integration_opportunities.description: Enhance cooling efficiency by integrating heat sink fins.
Improve cable management with custom routing guides.
Expand service cover area for easier maintenance.

verification_focus.description: Ensure proper insulation and separation between power and signal channels
Validate heat dissipation through inspection window
